meaning: n.有责任,有义务,可说明性
examples: Good management in the police cannot be divorced from accountability.
Then again, the air of accountability that the camera generates can also work in contractors' favor.
Words degraded to the margin have been justice, fairness, tolerance, proportionality and accountability.
Privatisation replaces democratic oversight and accountability with a contract culture that is a job creation scheme for lawyers.
But he brought with him an awful lot of jam tomorrow as well as accountability today.
